# Comprehensive Surgical & Hospitalization Insurance Guidelines (2026) ## Section 5: Inpatient & Outpatient Surgical Limits 5.1 Outpatient Surgical Procedures (e.g. Cataract surgery, Knee Arthroscopy, Appendectomy): Covered at 80% after deductible, up to a maximum payout of $3,500 per procedure. 5.2 Major Inpatient Surgery (e.g. Open Heart Surgery, Spinal Fusion, Joint Replacement): Requires pre-admission certification 72 hours prior to admission. Covered up to $25,000 lifetime benefit cap per individual member. 5.3 Intensive Care Unit (ICU) Room Rates: Covered up to $2,500 per day for a maximum of 14 continuous days. ## Section 6: Emergency Ambulance & Transportation 6.1 Ground Ambulance Services: Covered up to $800 per emergency trip to the nearest qualified facility. 6.2 Air Ambulance Services: Covered up to $5,000 per emergency evacuation when medically necessary and life-threatening. 6.3 Non-emergency medical transport is strictly non-covered. ## Section 7: Fraud Prevention & Audit Rules 7.1 Multiple claim submissions for the same service date by the same provider will trigger an automatic fraud risk flag. 7.2 Claims submitted within 30 days of initial policy enrollment are subjected to pre-existing condition audit.

# US Comprehensive Health & Emergency Insurance Policy Terms (2026 Edition) ## Section 1: General Coverage & Deductibles 1.1 Annual Individual Deductible: $1,000. Family Deductible: $2,500. 1.2 Out-of-Pocket Maximum: $5,000 per individual per calendar year. 1.3 In-network services are covered at 80% after meeting the annual deductible. Out-of-network services are covered at 50%. ## Section 2: Dental & Vision Coverage Limits 2.1 Routine Dental Cleanings: 100% covered, up to 2 cleanings per year. 2.2 Major Dental Treatments (e.g., Root Canals, Crowns, Orthodontics): Maximum payout cap of $500 per procedure, with a maximum annual dental benefit limit of $1,500 per member. 2.3 Vision & Eyewear: Annual allowance of $200 for prescription glasses or contact lenses. Routine eye exam covered up to $100 per year. ## Section 3: Hospitalization & Emergency Room Claims 3.1 Emergency Room (ER) Visits: Covered with a $250 copay per visit. Copay is waived if the member is admitted to the hospital within 24 hours. 3.2 Inpatient Hospital Stay: Requires prior authorization. Maximum covered room rate is $1,200 per day up to 30 continuous days per stay. 3.3 Prescription Medications: Tier 1 Generic ($15 copay), Tier 2 Preferred Brand ($40 copay), Tier 3 Non-Preferred Specialty ($80 copay). ## Section 4: Pre-Existing Conditions & Exclusions 4.1 Pre-existing conditions are fully covered after a 6-month continuous enrollment waiting period. 4.2 Experimental or unapproved medical treatments are strictly non-covered and ineligible for reimbursement. 4.3 Cosmetic surgeries are excluded unless medically necessary following an accident or trauma reconstruction.

Leave Policy Employees are entitled to the following types of leave per calendar year: Casual Leave: 12 days per year. Casual leave can be availed for short-term personal needs. It cannot be carried forward to the next year. Sick Leave: 10 days per year. Sick leave requires a medical certificate for absences exceeding 3 consecutive days. Earned Leave / Annual Leave: 18 days per year. Earned leave can be carried forward up to a maximum of 30 days. Maternity Leave: Female employees are entitled to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave as per statutory guidelines. Paternity Leave: Male employees are entitled to 5 days of paternity leave within 6 months of the child's birth. Public Holidays: Employees are entitled to all national public holidays declared by the government, typically 10-12 days per year. Leave Application: All leave must be applied for in advance through the HR portal, except for emergency sick leave which may be applied retroactively within 2 working days. Probation Period: Employees on probation (first 6 months) are entitled to 1 casual leave per month. Earned leave accrual begins after probation confirmation.

Employee Onboarding Policy Welcome to the company. This document outlines what new employees can expect during onboarding. Probation Period: All new employees undergo a probation period of 6 months. During this time, performance will be reviewed monthly. Confirmation of employment is subject to satisfactory performance. Joining Formalities: New employees must submit all required documents including educational certificates, previous employment letters, identity proof, address proof, and PAN card within 7 days of joining. Induction Program: A structured induction program runs for the first 2 weeks. It covers company culture, policies, department introductions, and tool access setup. System Access: IT accounts, email, and internal tools will be provisioned within 24 hours of joining. Employees must complete the mandatory security training within the first week. Buddy Program: Each new employee is assigned a buddy from their team to help with informal questions and settling in. ID Card and Access: Physical ID cards and office access cards are issued on the first day. Loss of ID card must be reported to HR and security immediately. Dress Code: Business casual is the standard dress code from Monday to Thursday. Fridays are casual dress days. Training and Development: Employees are encouraged to complete at least 40 hours of learning per year through the company's L&D platform.

Reimbursement Policy This policy covers the reimbursement of expenses incurred by employees in the course of official duties. Travel Reimbursement: Employees traveling for official purposes will be reimbursed for train, bus, or economy-class air travel. Travel must be pre-approved by the reporting manager and HR. Local Conveyance: Employees using personal vehicles for official local travel will be reimbursed at the rate of Rs. 5 per km for two-wheelers and Rs. 8 per km for four-wheelers. Accommodation: For outstation travel, accommodation reimbursement is capped based on employee grade: up to Rs. 3,000/night for junior staff, Rs. 5,000/night for mid-level, and Rs. 8,000/night for senior staff. Meal Allowance: A daily meal allowance of Rs. 500 per day is provided during official travel. Receipts must be submitted for claims above Rs. 250. Mobile and Internet: Employees in designated roles receive a monthly mobile and internet reimbursement of up to Rs. 1,000 upon submission of bills. Medical Reimbursement: Employees are eligible for medical reimbursement of up to Rs. 15,000 per year for self and dependents. Claims must be submitted with original prescriptions and bills. Claim Process: All reimbursement claims must be submitted through the expense portal within 30 days of incurring the expense. Claims submitted after 30 days may not be processed. Receipts: Original receipts or GST invoices are mandatory for all claims above Rs. 100.

Work From Home Policy The company supports flexible working arrangements through a structured work from home policy. Eligibility: Permanent employees who have completed their probation period are eligible for work from home privileges. Employees on probation are not eligible except in special medical or emergency circumstances approved by HR. WFH Days: Eligible employees may work from home up to 2 days per week. The specific days should be agreed upon with the reporting manager to ensure team coverage. Approval Process: WFH days must be planned in advance and communicated to the team. Ad hoc WFH requests must be approved by the reporting manager by 9:00 AM on the day. Core Hours: Employees working from home must be available and reachable during core hours of 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Attendance on video calls and Slack/Teams is mandatory. Equipment and Connectivity: Employees are responsible for ensuring a stable internet connection and a suitable working environment. The company does not provide home internet reimbursement unless the employee is in a field-designated role. Data Security: All work from home employees must follow the data security policy strictly. Use of personal devices for company work is not permitted without prior IT approval and MDM enrollment. Performance: WFH privileges may be revoked if there is a pattern of missed deliverables, unresponsiveness during core hours, or violation of data security policies. Exceptions: Certain roles that require physical presence, such as operations and facilities staff, are not eligible for WFH unless explicitly stated in their employment terms.
